data.sentence: Hi, thanks for calling Aegis Cloud Security support, this is Elena Vasquez, I have my colleague David Kim on the line with me as well — how can we help you today?
Yeah, hi, this is Isaac Brennan, I'm the infrastructure lead over at Trailhead Marketplace, and I'm going to be completely honest with you — I am not happy right now.
Of course, Isaac, I completely understand — can you walk us through what you're seeing?

What I'm seeing is nothing. Literally nothing. Our Detect alerts have not fired once since yesterday evening and we are a retail platform, we have thousands of transactions moving through our infrastructure every hour, and the idea that zero threats have been flagged in that window is — that's not realistic, that's a broken product.
Okay, so just to make sure I understand — you're saying alerts are completely silent, like no notifications at all, or are you seeing events in the dashboard but just no alert triggers?

Both. The dashboard is — it's showing stale data. The last event I can see processed in the UI is from, I wrote it down, 6:47 PM yesterday. It's almost 10 AM right now. That's fifteen-plus hours of nothing.
Okay, Isaac, I — I want to be upfront with you, and I'm going to pull up your tenant right now, but I do need to flag something that may be relevant here.
What does that mean, may be relevant — either it's relevant or it isn't.

You're right, I'll just say it — we are currently dealing with a platform-wide issue affecting Aegis Detect's event processing pipeline. It started sometime yesterday evening and our engineering team is actively working on it.
I'm sorry, a platform-wide issue? As in this is not just us?
That's correct, it appears to be broader than just your tenant, yes.

So why — why did I have to call in to find this out? Why was there no proactive communication? We have a status page subscription, we have an account manager, I have received zero emails, zero SMS alerts, nothing.
That's — that's a completely fair point and I don't have a good answer for you on the communication side, Isaac, that's something we'll need to escalate separately. I'm really sorry about that.

Do you understand what this means for us? We're a marketplace, we have PCI obligations, we have fraud monitoring that depends on Detect firing in near real-time. If someone has been running a card skimming operation through our checkout since 6 PM yesterday and we had no visibility — I don't even want to finish that sentence.

I hear you, and I want you to know we are taking this seriously. Can you confirm — are your Aegis Protect backups still running normally? Just want to make sure we understand the full scope of what's affected for you.
As far as I can tell, Protect seems fine, Identity seems fine. It's purely Detect that's dead.
Okay, that's consistent with what we're seeing on our end. The issue is isolated to the event processing pipeline within Detect.
Isolated — I would not use the word isolated when you've essentially blinded every customer's threat monitoring. What is the ETA on a fix? Give me something concrete.

I — I want to give you a concrete number, I really do. Right now engineering is working through the root cause analysis, we believe it's a cascading failure in the event ingestion layer but I don't have a confirmed resolution time I can commit to right now.
A cascading failure. Great. So this is a fundamental architectural problem, not a config issue, not a quick restart.
We're not ready to characterize the full scope of it on this call, but yes, it's not a simple fix. I want to be honest with you rather than give you a two-hour ETA that I can't back up.

You know, I advocated internally to go with Aegis over SentinelShield. I had my VP breathing down my neck about the price point and I said no, these guys have the better platform, the better support. I'm going to look like an idiot right now.
Isaac, I understand, and I — I genuinely appreciate that you went to bat for us, which is exactly why this situation is unacceptable and I won't try to dress it up as anything else.
What can you actually do for me right now? Not in two hours, not when engineering gets back to you — right now, on this call.

A few things — I'm escalating this case to P1 priority immediately and getting your account tagged for direct engineering contact. I'm also going to get your account manager looped in so your leadership gets a direct line for updates, not just status page.

And in the meantime we'd strongly recommend reviewing your fraud transaction logs manually through whatever secondary tooling you have, just as a stop-gap until Detect is back up. I know that's not a satisfying answer but —
Yeah, no kidding it's not a satisfying answer. We're paying for an automated threat detection platform so we don't have to do manual log reviews.
You're absolutely right and I'm not suggesting it's equivalent — it's purely a precautionary measure while the team works the fix.
I want a full incident report. I want it in writing, I want root cause, I want timeline, I want what you're doing to make sure this never happens again — and I want it before end of week.
That's completely reasonable and I'm documenting that request right now as a formal deliverable tied to this case.

And I'll tell you right now — depending on how this is handled over the next 24 hours, we're going to have a very serious conversation about the future of this contract. My leadership is not going to let this slide.

I understand, Isaac. I'm not going to make you promises I can't keep but I can tell you this case is my personal priority right now and I will be your point of contact until this is resolved. You'll hear from me within two hours with an update, even if that update is just where we are in the process.
Two hours. I'm holding you to that.
Understood. I'm going to grab your direct line and email before we close out — can you confirm the best way to reach you?
Cell is best. It's on the account. And Elena — I really hope this gets turned around because I don't want to be shopping platforms in the middle of Q2. But I will if I have to.
I hear you. We're going to do everything we can. Thank you for calling in, Isaac, I know this wasn't the conversation any of us wanted to have.
